hypothesis: If molecular generation is steered by adding concept-based activation vectors—derived from a frozen Concept Bottleneck Model trained to encode interpretable, orthogonal property-specific embeddings into a frozen LLM's hidden states—then the resulting conditional molecular generation will achieve lower property prediction error and higher generative efficiency compared to baseline steering and fine-tuning methods, because disentangled, property-aligned steering vectors provide more precise and generalizable control over the LLM's generative process without altering its pretrained chemical knowledge.
